skill.name: kling-3-0
skill.description: Use Kling 3.0 on RunComfy to generate text-to-video and image-to-video clips across Standard, Pro, and 4K tiers. The skill helps select the correct endpoint, prepare prompts, configure duration, aspect ratio, audio, and image inputs, then invoke the local RunComfy CLI.
skill.summary: Generate Kling 3.0 videos with RunComfy using text or image inputs.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found no prompt injection, malware, or repository code that performs hidden execution. Most command, weak-cryptography, and traversal hits are Markdown false positives; the real risks are expected external CLI execution, RunComfy network submission, token handling, and generated-file downloads.

medium_findings.title: External RunComfy CLI Invocation
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to install and run the RunComfy CLI, including multiple `runcomfy run` commands. This is expected for the skill purpose, but it executes an external tool and submits user prompts to a third-party service, so users should review commands and credentials before use.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands clearly invoke `runcomfy` and require login or a token. The commands support the stated video-generation function, so this is an elevated operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party API Submission and Remote Downloads
medium_findings.description: The skill documents that the CLI POSTs requests to the RunComfy Model API, polls for completion, and downloads result URLs from RunComfy domains. This creates expected network exposure for prompts, image URLs, bearer tokens, and generated media.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly describes API submission, polling, result downloads, and outbound endpoint domains. The network behavior is transparent and aligned with the RunComfy integration.

medium_findings.title: Local Token Storage and Environment Token Use
medium_findings.description: The skill documents that `runcomfy login` stores an API token under the user configuration directory, and CI can use `RUNCOMFY_TOKEN`. Mode 0600 is noted, but token handling remains sensitive and should be disclosed.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and environment variable are explicitly documented. The file permission note reduces severity, but credential storage and environment token use remain relevant security considerations.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern Matches Are Mostly Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby backtick, weak cryptography, and path traversal alerts are not confirmed as executable code in this repository. They occur in Markdown prose, inline code, endpoint names, placeholder URLs, or pricing examples, and no evidence of malware, prompt injection, or unauthorized exfiltration was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is documentation-only Markdown and the flagged snippets are examples or prose. Confidence is below very high because the marketplace still depends on the external RunComfy CLI behavior at runtime.

dangerous_patterns.title: External RunComfy CLI Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs the agent to install and run the RunComfy CLI, including multiple `runcomfy run` commands. This is expected for the skill purpose, but it executes an external tool and submits user prompts to a third-party service, so users should review commands and credentials before use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The documented commands clearly invoke `runcomfy` and require login or a token. The commands support the stated video-generation function, so this is an elevated operational risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party API Submission and Remote Downloads
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents that the CLI POSTs requests to the RunComfy Model API, polls for completion, and downloads result URLs from RunComfy domains. This creates expected network exposure for prompts, image URLs, bearer tokens, and generated media.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly describes API submission, polling, result downloads, and outbound endpoint domains. The network behavior is transparent and aligned with the RunComfy integration.

content.value_statement: Video teams need a repeatable way to choose the right Kling 3.0 endpoint and prepare valid inputs. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through RunComfy CLI commands for text-to-video and image-to-video generation.
actual_capabilities: Routes requests across six Kling 3.0 endpoints for Standard, Pro, and 4K tiers.
Supports text-to-video and image-to-video workflows through the RunComfy CLI.
Helps choose tier, duration, aspect ratio, audio, seed, and prompt guidance settings.
Explains multi-shot prompt structure and identity anchors for consistent subjects.
Documents source image and tail image options for image-to-video generation.
Describes CLI prerequisites, login options, output directories, and RunComfy endpoints.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and a RunComfy account or API token.
Sends prompts, image URLs, and generation requests to RunComfy infrastructure.
Image-to-video inputs must be publicly fetchable HTTPS image URLs.
Each generation is limited to 15 seconds, and 4K outputs can be costly and large.

use_cases.title: Prototype Social Video Variants
use_cases.description: Generate short vertical or square drafts with the Standard tier before selecting final creative directions.

use_cases.title: Create Premium Brand Clips
use_cases.description: Prepare Pro or 4K prompts with camera motion, shot sequencing, audio direction, and stable character identity.

use_cases.title: Animate Product and Character Images
use_cases.description: Use image-to-video inputs to animate a reference image while preserving the subject, clothing, and composition.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Draft Video
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a low-cost first pass from a simple scene idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Kling 3.0 Standard to create a 5 second 16:9 text-to-video draft of this scene: [describe scene]. Keep it simple and production-ready.

prompt_templates.title: Social Short Variant
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a vertical short with clear motion and audio direction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 9:16 Kling 3.0 Standard prompt for a short social video about [topic]. Include camera movement, atmosphere, and optional audio direction.

prompt_templates.title: Image Animation
prompt_templates.scenario: Animate a product, portrait, or concept image from a public URL.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Kling V3.0 Pro image-to-video for this image URL: [URL]. Preserve the main subject and add subtle motion, lighting changes, and camera movement.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Multi-Shot 4K Sequence
prompt_templates.scenario: Plan a finished cinematic clip with multiple shots and synchronized audio.
prompt_templates.prompt: Prepare a Kling V3.0 4K multi-shot prompt for [campaign or scene]. Include shot numbers, transitions, identity anchors, audio direction, duration, and aspect ratio.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a fast cafe advertisement draft for mobile social channels.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends the Standard text-to-video endpoint, prepares a 9:16 prompt with espresso motion and cafe ambience, and notes the lower iteration cost.

output_examples.input: A user provides a product image URL and wants a polished hero animation.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects Pro image-to-video, preserves the product shape and label, adds controlled camera movement, and suggests audio only if needed.

output_examples.input: A user needs a high-resolution brand film master.
output_examples.output: The assistant chooses the 4K tier, structures a multi-shot sequence, includes audio direction, and warns about cost, file size, and duration limits.

best_practices: Start with Standard tier drafts before spending on Pro or 4K generations.
Front-load camera movement, shot order, subject identity, and audio direction in the prompt.
Review RunComfy commands, output paths, and token handling before submitting a generation.
anti_patterns: Do not request unsupported aspect ratios or clips longer than 15 seconds in one call.
Do not use image-to-video with private local files or URLs that RunComfy cannot fetch.
Do not send sensitive prompts, private imagery, or confidential brand assets without approval.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It helps generate Kling 3.0 videos through RunComfy, using text prompts or public image URLs.

faq.question: Does it require a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented workflow requires the RunComfy CLI plus login or a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN value.

faq.question: Which Kling 3.0 tiers are supported?
faq.answer: It covers Standard, Pro, and 4K tiers for both text-to-video and image-to-video modes.

faq.question: Can it generate audio?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents the generate_audio option and explains cost differences across tiers.

faq.question: Can it use local images?
faq.answer: No. Image-to-video workflows require publicly fetchable HTTPS image URLs.

faq.question: Is the skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses an external CLI, third-party network calls, and token handling.
